month, when Mr.. Boyd, then council
man, refused to sign his committees
report giving the printing to the pa
per with the union label. Three times
Mr. Boyd refused to vote for the la
bel. finally resigning his seat rather
than go on record. His action re-
suited i'm the following resolutions
against him. still standing, by the
trades council:


Whereas, 1t has been the object
and aim of organized labor of Spo-
kane to receive recognition from the
city administration in the way of
the advancement of the union label
and the employment of union labor
on the public improvements of the
city. Therefore be it


Resolved. That we especially con-
denim the manner in which Colonel
Frank Boyd lodged the issue and as
sisted in. postponing the issue until
after his resignation had taken eff
feet"
